message:
I have a friend who keeps a laminated storm checklist in her kitchen drawer. Her kids used to roll their eyes about it. Then a surprise ice storm hit last winter, and while the rest of us were scrambling at the last minute, she calmly made sure everyone had what they needed—flashlights with fresh batteries, a way to stay warm, snacks that didn't need electricity. Her kids stopped eye-rolling real fast.

I realized that being "over-prepared" isn't about anxiety or paranoia—it's actually pretty freeing. You get to relax *because* you've thought things through ahead of time. No white-knuckle panic at 11 p.m. when the forecast changes.

I'm not suggesting you need a laminated checklist (though honestly, kind of clever), just that a little seasonal weather prep is kind of like bringing an umbrella—it's not stressful, it just makes you feel more ready.

What's your go-to storm-readiness move? Are you a planner or more of a "we'll figure it out" person?
